headline: Super Micro Deepens Mirantis Ties To Standardize Open Source AI Clouds
summary: Super Micro Computer (NasdaqGS:SMCI) is expanding its ecosystem partnerships to automate AI cloud infrastructure with Mirantis. The collaboration focuses on integrating open source platforms, including k0rdent AI, with NVIDIA based GPU cloud environments. The move targets sovereign AI and hybrid GPU cloud use cases, with an emphasis on operational automation and reduced manual provisioning. For investors watching AI infrastructure, this partnership illustrates how NasdaqGS:SMCI is...

headline: Adobe Links Semrush Deal And NVIDIA Alliance To AI Marketing Future
summary: Adobe, ticker NasdaqGS:ADBE, plans to acquire marketing platform Semrush for US$1.9b. The company also announced an expanded partnership with NVIDIA focused on next generation Firefly models and AI agent workflows. These moves are aimed at deepening Adobe's AI driven marketing and search capabilities beyond its core creative tools. For you as an investor, this sits at the crossroads of creative software, search marketing, and generative AI. Adobe already plays a central role in content...

headline: Arm to Sell Its Own Chips, Eyeing Sales Goal of $15 Billion
summary: (Bloomberg) -- Arm Holdings Plc, which made its name licensing technology to semiconductor makers, will begin selling its own chips for the first time, adding a business that it expects to generate about $15 billion annually within five years. Meta Platforms Inc. will be the first major customer for the company’s chip, called an AGI CPU, Arm said Tuesday at an event in San Francisco. The product will have as many as 136 cores — a measure of processing power — and draw 300 watts of electricity, A

headline: NextEra Energy Links AI Factories To Grid As Valuation Stays Rich
summary: NextEra Energy (NYSE:NEE) is working with NVIDIA and Emerald AI to build grid integrated AI factories that tie computing capacity directly to power infrastructure. headline: Alibaba Chip Push With XuanTie C950 Recasts AI And Cloud Narrative
summary: Alibaba Group Holding (NYSE:BABA) has introduced the XuanTie C950 RISC-V AI chip, developed by its Damo Academy. The new processor is built for AI inference and cloud workloads and is positioned to compete with chips from Nvidia, Huawei, Arm, and Intel. The XuanTie C950 is linked to Alibaba’s T-Head unit, which is preparing for mass production and is being discussed as a potential separate listing. Alibaba Group Holding, trading at $125.48, is adding a new angle to its AI story with...
